generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for 3D environmental perception in the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle, providing raw point cloud data for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing stereo vision and depth mapping for environmental perception in the autonomous vehicle system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensors collecting vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation for real-time monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuatorFeedback : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuation feedback sensors monitoring motor, steering, and servo commands to validate physical system responses."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to the Digital Twin Entity for processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to the Digital Twin Entity for processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data (speed, position, orientation) directly acquired from on-board sensors and transmitted to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Actuation feedback data (motor, steering, servo commands) transmitted to validate physical system responses."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorFeedback
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ProcessedLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Pre-processed LiDAR point cloud data converted into actionable object tracks and environmental representations via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedDepthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth camera data integrated with LiDAR data for enhanced environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SensorFusionEnabler
]

instance StandardizedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data (speed, position, orientation) translated into standardized units for system compatibility."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ControlCommandFeedback : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Actuation feedback data processed to validate physical system responses against commanded actions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationValidationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks generated from processed LiDAR and depth data for real-time obstacle detection and avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARPointCloud, ProcessedDepthData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Environmental representation combining processed sensor data for 3D visualization and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData, StandardizedVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalModelingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component performing point cloud clustering and tracking to extract meaningful object representations from raw LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:enables LiDARProcessingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component serializing and aligning message types, ensuring compatibility across the system with unit conversions for all data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network enabling modular and real-time communication between software components within the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables IntraEntityCommunicationService
]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network facilitating inter-entity communication between the Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity for sensor data transmission."
    DTDFVocab:enables InterEntityCommunicationService
]

instance VisualizationPresentationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Presentation FE for pre-processing and display optimization of processed data for 3D visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SensorFusionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component integrating heterogeneous data streams (LiDAR + depth camera) into a unified representation for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorFusionService
]

instance ObjectTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component generating and maintaining object tracks from processed sensor data for real-time obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance EnvironmentalModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component creating environmental representations from processed sensor data for visualization and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnvironmentalModelingService
]

instance ActuationValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component validating physical system responses against commanded actions using actuation feedback data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationValidationService
]

instance JetsonOrinNanoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano embedded AI/control processor for real-time sensor fusion and dynamic obstacle avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:enables EdgeComputingService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) middleware for inter-process communication between sensors, actuators, and AI modules."
    DTDFVocab:enables MiddlewareIntegrationService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for model-based design (MBD) of ACC algorithms, co-simulated with the Digital Twin for validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables AlgorithmValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance LiDARProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing clustering and tracking of LiDAR point clouds to generate object tracks and environmental representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiDARObjectTrackInsight
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring data compatibility through serialization, alignment, and unit conversion of all data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

instance IntraEntityCommunicationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time communication between software components within the Digital Twin Entity via ROS2 network."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentInteroperabilityInsight
]

instance InterEntityCommunicationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating sensor data transmission from the Physical Twin Entity to the Digital Twin Entity over Wi-Fi."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service rendering processed data (object tracks, vehicle state, projected paths) in 3D for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance SensorFusionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating heterogeneous data streams (LiDAR + depth camera) into a unified representation for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedSensorDataInsight
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating and maintaining object tracks from processed sensor data for real-time obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObstacleDetectionInsight
]

instance EnvironmentalModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating environmental representations from processed sensor data for visualization and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalRepresentationInsight
]

instance ActuationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating physical system responses against commanded actions using actuation feedback data."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemResponseValidationInsight
]

instance EdgeComputingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling dynamic workload distribution between the physical twin and Digital Twin infrastructure for latency-critical tasks."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputationalOffloadingInsight
]

instance MiddlewareIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing inter-process communication between sensors, actuators, and AI modules via ROS2 middleware."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemIntegrationInsight
]

instance AlgorithmValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating ACC algorithms through co-simulation between the Digital Twin and MATLAB/Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:provides AlgorithmValidationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling teleoperational commands and fail-safe interventions via Wi-Fi communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance LiDARObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing actionable object tracks and environmental representations from processed LiDAR data."
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring data consistency through standardized formats and unit conversions."
]

instance ComponentInteroperabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling seamless communication between software components within the Digital Twin Entity."
]

instance DataTransmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring reliable transmission of sensor data from the Physical Twin Entity to the Digital Twin Entity."
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time 3D visualization of processed data for monitoring and analysis."
]

instance UnifiedSensorDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight integrating heterogeneous sensor data streams into a unified representation for analysis."
]

instance ObstacleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time obstacle detection and tracking for autonomous navigation."
]

instance EnvironmentalRepresentationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight creating detailed environmental representations for path planning and decision-making."
]

instance SystemResponseValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating physical system responses against commanded actions using actuation feedback."
]

instance ComputationalOffloadingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling dynamic workload distribution between physical and digital twin infrastructures."
]

instance SystemIntegrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring seamless integration between sensors, actuators, and AI modules via ROS2 middleware."
]

instance AlgorithmValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating ACC algorithms through co-simulation with MATLAB/Simulink."
]

instance RemoteControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling teleoperational commands and remote management of the autonomous vehicle."
]

instance FailSafeInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action triggering emergency interventions (e.g., braking) when obstacles are detected via LiDAR."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller for the lower chassis, converting desired speed inputs into motor voltage commands for steering and speed control."
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity representing the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with computational and sensor modules."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuatorFeedback, VedderElectronicSpeedController
]

// C1: Digital Twin Entity
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity representing the digital mirror of the physical autonomous vehicle for real-time monitoring and analysis."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2NetworkEnabler, VisualizationPresentationEnabler,
                SensorFusionEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler, EnvironmentalModelingEnabler, ActuationValidationEnabler,
                JetsonOrinNanoEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance AutonomousVehicleSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle system for adaptive cruise control applications."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of control commands from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, enabling real-time adjustments to the vehicle’s behavior. Key control flows include:
1. **Command Types**:
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) commands**: Enabling/disabling ACC, modifying desired speed, or triggering emergency braking.
   - **Actuation commands**: Direct control signals for motor, steering, and servo systems (e.g., throttle adjustments, steering angle corrections).
2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are serialized and translated via the **Data Translation** component to ensure compatibility with physical system interfaces.
   - The **Actuation FE** processes these commands to generate executable control signals (e.g., PWM for motor actuation).
3. **Validation & Authentication**:
   - Implicit validation occurs during data translation (e.g., unit conversion checks, message type alignment).
   - No explicit authentication mechanisms (e.g., digital signatures) are described in the document.
4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - Physical system responses (e.g., motor RPM, steering angle) are fed back to the **Digital Twin Entity** via sensor data streams, closing the control loop.
   - The **Actuation FE** monitors feedback to confirm command execution (e.g., verifying motor speed matches the commanded value).
5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Potential conflicts (e.g., competing ACC and manual steering commands) may be resolved via priority-based arbitration within the **Controlling FE**.
6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - Confirmed through sensor feedback loops (e.g., LiDAR tracks validating steering corrections) or explicit acknowledgments from the **Actuation FE**.
7. **Communication Path**:
   - Commands propagate from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity** over the same **Wi-Fi network** used for sensor data transmission (transport layer protocol unspecified).
   - Intra-entity communication within the **Digital Twin Entity** uses **ROS2** for modular control logic execution (e.g., *Controlling FE* → *Actuation FE*).

**Note**: Specific message formats (e.g., ROS2 message types), validation rules, or conflict resolution strategies are not provided in the document.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter clock synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services but implies real-time constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication within the **Physical Twin Entity**. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **ROS2-based execution framework**, where each Function Element (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package, ensuring deterministic timing for inter-node communication (e.g., between the *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components within the *User Interface FE*)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **entity-based orchestration pattern** derived from ISO 23247 RA, where **Function Elements (FEs)** are mapped to ROS2 packages and deployed across distributed hardware (e.g., FEs for *Physical Twin Entity* on embedded systems, *User Entity* on user-facing devices). Resource management is implicit in the **Physical Twin Entity**, which abstracts inherent system resources (compute, sensors, actuators) and general automotive functions (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). System-wide coordination relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model** for inter-entity communication, with no centralized controller. The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by **expanding the Physical Twin Entity’s scope** beyond resource-specific functionality to include general automotive logic, potentially impacting **ownership and engineering processes**. The *User Interface FE*’s split into visualization/control subcomponents introduces **modularity** within the *User Entity*, while the unaligned *Data Storage* component suggests **post-hoc integration** outside the standard’s FE hierarchy."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces quantitative fidelity requirements, including a **network latency constraint of at most 100 ms** and **clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms** to ensure real-time coordination between physical and digital components. Validation and verification methods are not explicitly detailed, but the system incorporates a **human-in-the-loop** approach for monitoring and control. Uncertainty sources, error detection mechanisms, or formal quality assurance processes are not specified in the provided document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed to support real-time communication between entities and software components, with the following specifications:
1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-entity (SW components)**: **ROS2** (Robot Operating System 2) is used for communication between software components within the **Digital Twin Entity** and **Physical Twin Entity**. ROS2 provides a modular, publish-subscribe architecture for data exchange.
   - **Inter-entity (Physical ↔ Digital)**: Communication occurs over a **Wi-Fi network** (transport layer protocol unspecified; the document cuts off at 'using U').
2. **Network Topology & Architecture**:
   - **Decentralized deployment**: The **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** are deployed on separate hardware components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson devices), with data flowing between them via dedicated communication channels.
   - **Hierarchical structure**: Sensor data from the **Physical Twin Entity** is aggregated and processed before being transmitted to the **Digital Twin Entity** for visualization and control.
3. **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
   - **Latency**: The system requires **<100ms** end-to-end latency for real-time monitoring and control (implied by the need for ACC and 3D visualization updates).
   - **Bandwidth**: Not explicitly quantified in the document, but high-bandwidth requirements are inferred for LiDAR point cloud transmission (e.g., millions of points per second).
4. **Reliability & Redundancy**:
   - **No explicit redundancy mechanisms** are described (e.g., backup communication paths or failover protocols).
   - Reliability is partially ensured by the **Data Translation** component’s role in validating and aligning message types.
5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No security protocols (e.g., encryption, access control) are mentioned for network communication.

**Note**: The document does not specify:
- The exact transport protocol (e.g., UDP vs. TCP) for inter-entity communication.
- Quality of Service (QoS) settings for ROS2 or Wi-Fi communication.
- Network segmentation or isolation strategies.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is optimized for edge computing, with the following technical details:
1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge deployment**: The system is deployed on **NVIDIA Jetson NX** and **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** devices, which provide the computational power for real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, 3D visualization).
   - **No cloud or on-premise components** are mentioned; the architecture is hardware-centric and localized.
2. **Computational Requirements**:
   - **High-performance processing**: Required for tasks such as point cloud clustering, object tracking, and 3D rendering (e.g., CUDA-accelerated GPUs on Jetson devices).
   - **Real-time constraints**: The system must handle low-latency operations (e.g., <100ms for ACC control loops).
3. **Storage Requirements**:
   - **External storage**: Used for data collection, reporting, and long-term storage of sensor logs (e.g., LiDAR scans, vehicle state history).
   - **No specific storage media** (e.g., SSD, HDD) or capacity details are provided.
4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit scalability strategies (e.g., horizontal scaling, load balancing) are described.
5. **Deployment Models**:
   - **Modular deployment**: Each entity (**Physical Twin**, **Digital Twin**, **User**) is deployed on separate hardware components, with clear separation of concerns (e.g., sensor data acquisition vs. visualization).
   - **ROS2-based modularity**: Software components are deployed as ROS2 nodes, enabling dynamic reconfiguration and updates.
6. **Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Hardware redundancy**: Not explicitly mentioned, but the use of dedicated Jetson devices for critical functions (e.g., ACC control) implies redundancy in some form.
   - **Fault tolerance**: No explicit mechanisms (e.g., watchdog timers, automatic failover) are described.
   - **Data persistence**: Ensured through external storage for reporting and historical analysis.

**Note**: The document does not specify:
- Use of virtualization (e.g., containers, VMs) or orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes).
- Specific operating systems (e.g., Linux RTOS) or deployment automation frameworks.
- Geographical distribution or multi-node clustering strategies.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system complies with the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Architecture**: A standardized decomposition into **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with clearly delineated responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: While the standard does not mandate specific protocols, it implies compatibility with **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between entities, as noted for the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**.
  - **Certification and Compliance**: The framework aligns with **manufacturing digital twin best practices** but does not detail certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443). Compliance is inferred through adherence to the **entity-based RA** and **information exchange guidelines** (Part 4 of ISO 23247).
  - **Domain-Specific Specifications**: The standard is tailored for **manufacturing use cases**, covering physical elements (equipment, facilities), processes, and personnel. It does not address sector-agnostic standards (e.g., GAIA-X, IIoT reference architectures) but provides a foundational template for interoperability across manufacturing subdomains."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents reference safety-critical systems in the context of **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** as per **ISO 15622:2018**, which outlines performance requirements and test procedures for such systems. However, no explicit mention of **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles) or its **Automotive Safety Integrity Level (ASIL)** classifications, **Hazard and Risk Assessment (HARA)**, or **fail-safe mechanisms** is found. The **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0** standard is cited for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**, specifically to ensure **network latency of at most 100 ms** for service requirements, but does not address **end-to-end security protocols, authentication systems, or threat mitigation strategies**. The **safety-critical nature** of the system is implied by the ACC context, but no technical details on **cybersecurity protocols, access control, or risk assessment approaches** are provided."
]
